{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"Raise capacity to a small two-stage micro-ResNet and align the learning-rate schedule to the fixed step budget with AdamW + OneCycleLR, which is a stronger use of the 256-step training window than the current tiny single-block baseline.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- Architecture hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
DEPTH = 1
BASE_CHANNELS = 8
CHANNEL_MULT = 2
USE_BATCHNORM = True
DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0

DEPTH = 2

DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0
FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- Optimizer hyperparameters ----------------------------------------------
OPTIMIZER = "adam"

MOMENTUM = 0.9
ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"# --- Optimizer hyperparameters ----------------------------------------------

MOMENTUM = 0.9
ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- LR schedule hyperparameters --------------------------------------------
USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False
WARMUP_EPOCHS = 2
LR_DECAY_FACTOR = 0.1

BATCH_SIZE = 64
NUM_WORKERS = 0","new":"# --- Batch / data hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
BATCH_SIZE = 128
NUM_WORKERS = 0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"class ResidualBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, channels: int, dropout: float = 0.0, use_bn: bool = True):
        super().__init__()
        bias = not use_bn
        layers1: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        layers2: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if use_bn:
            layers1.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
            layers2.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        self.conv1 = nn.Sequential(*layers1)
        self.conv2 = nn.Sequential(*layers2)
        self.activation = nn.ReLU(inplace=True)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout2d(dropout) if dropout > 0 else nn.Identity()

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        residual = x
        out = self.activation(self.conv1(x))
        out = self.dropout(out)
        out = self.conv2(out)
        out = self.activation(out + residual)
        return out","new":"class ResidualBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        in_channels: int,
        out_channels: int,
        stride: int = 1,
        dropout: float = 0.0,
        use_bn: bool = True,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        bias = not use_bn
        layers1: list[nn.Module] = [
            nn.Conv2d(in_channels, out_channels, 3, stride=stride, padding=1, bias=bias)
        ]
        layers2: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(out_channels, out_channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if use_bn:
            layers1.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels))
            layers2.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels))
        self.conv1 = nn.Sequential(*layers1)
        self.conv2 = nn.Sequential(*layers2)
        if in_channels == out_channels and stride == 1:
            self.shortcut = nn.Identity()
        else:
            shortcut_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(in_channels, out_channels, 1, stride=stride, bias=bias)]
            if use_bn:
                shortcut_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels))
            self.shortcut = nn.Sequential(*shortcut_layers)
        self.activation = nn.ReLU(inplace=True)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout2d(dropout) if dropout > 0 else nn.Identity()

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        residual = self.shortcut(x)
        out = self.activation(self.conv1(x))
        out = self.dropout(out)
        out = self.conv2(out)
        out = self.activation(out + residual)
        return out","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        stem_channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        stage_channels = BASE_CHANNELS * CHANNEL_MULT
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM

        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, stem_channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(stem_channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)

        self.stage1 = self._make_stage(stem_channels, stem_channels, DEPTH)
        downsample_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(stem_channels, stage_channels, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            downsample_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(stage_channels))
        downsample_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.downsample = nn.Sequential(*downsample_layers)
        self.stage2 = self._make_stage(stage_channels, stage_channels, DEPTH)

        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(stage_channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def _make_stage(self, in_channels: int, out_channels: int, depth: int) -> nn.Sequential:
        blocks: list[nn.Module] = [
            ResidualBlock(in_channels, out_channels, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE)
        ]
        for _ in range(max(1, depth) - 1):
            blocks.append(ResidualBlock(out_channels, out_channels, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE))
        return nn.Sequential(*blocks)

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.stage1(x)
        x = self.downsample(x)
        x = self.stage2(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=total_steps)","new":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    total_steps = max(1, total_steps)
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=total_steps,
        pct_start=0.1,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=100.0,
    )","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Push a bit more model capacity into the already successful stage-wise micro-ResNet and make AdamW slightly more aggressive for the short fixed-step budget; this is the lowest-risk next move after the first improvement.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- Architecture hyperparameters -------------------------------------------

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Add light label smoothing to regularize the short CIFAR-10 run without changing the already-working optimization and architecture stack.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Reduce the smoothing strength a bit after the previous gain; 0.1 may be slightly too strong for this short fixed-step run, so 0.05 is a safer regularization point.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Restore the better 0.1 label smoothing and make the OneCycle tail less aggressive so the last chunk of this short run keeps learning instead of freezing out too early.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        final_div_factor=100.0,","new":"        final_div_factor=20.0,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"Return to the longer OneCycle tail that was strongest so far and make AdamW less reactive with the standard beta2 setting, which is a plausible stability gain for the same short budget.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        final_div_factor=20.0,","new":"        final_div_factor=100.0,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"Roll back to the best backbone configuration seen so far and try a smaller batch for more gradient noise and lower per-step cost; this is the next credible way to move past the current plateau.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- Architecture hyperparameters -------------------------------------------

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Batch 64 was clearly too noisy here, so try a middle batch size and a less conservative OneCycle start; that is the most plausible unexplored adjustment from the current state.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        div_factor=10.0,","new":"        div_factor=5.0,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Restore the best batch size and OneCycle scale, then spend less of the short 256-step budget warming up so the optimizer reaches its useful range earlier.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 96","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        pct_start=0.1,","new":"        pct_start=0.05,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        div_factor=5.0,","new":"        div_factor=10.0,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The short-budget OneCycle setup seems to benefit from a briefer warmup, so shift a few more steps into the useful part of the cycle and test whether the current gain can be extended.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        pct_start=0.05,","new":"        pct_start=0.03,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"Restore the better warmup fraction and trim weight decay a bit; the current short-run regime looks more likely to benefit from less regularization than from additional warmup shrinkage.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        pct_start=0.03,","new":"        pct_start=0.05,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The last gain came from reducing weight decay again, so continue that trend with a slightly lighter decoupled penalty while keeping the otherwise best-known setup fixed.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-6","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The last two gains both came from lowering weight decay, so continue that sweep to the no-decay limit and check whether the model is still under-regularized.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-6","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"With regularization already minimized, a slightly smaller peak learning rate is the most plausible remaining knob to reduce oscillation and squeeze out a bit more validation loss.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"],"rationale":"Restore the best-known peak LR and tiny decay, then probe a slightly shorter warmup than the current best since 0.03 was too short and 0.05 may not be exact.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-6","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        pct_start=0.05,","new":"        pct_start=0.04,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The current setup looks most sensitive to the late-cycle refinement phase, so lower the final learning rate a bit more and see whether the last stretch can sharpen the best configuration.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        final_div_factor=100.0,","new":"        final_div_factor=150.0,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The best runs clustered around a 0.05 warmup and a 100x final decay; try nudging warmup slightly longer while restoring the stronger tail decay to see if the optimum is just above the current setting.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        pct_start=0.04,","new":"        pct_start=0.06,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        final_div_factor=150.0,","new":"        final_div_factor=100.0,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The 0.06 warmup regressed, so revert to the 0.05 setting that produced the best verified loss in this run.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        pct_start=0.06,","new":"        pct_start=0.05,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}
